episode_name: Iraqi invasion and occupation of Kuwait (pre-air-war phase)
theater_description: The relevant civilian theater is occupied Kuwait as a whole, with large outflows of Kuwaiti citizens and foreign workers after the invasion. Violence mode is occupation repression, detention, deportation, and scattered armed resistance rather than coalition bombing or maneuver warfare.
geometry_method_note: Treat the occupied emirate as the civilian footprint; any eventual denominator must reduce for the very large refugee outflow rather than use Kuwait's full prewar resident population.
military_area_note: A future military denominator would need Iraqi occupation forces and any operationally active Kuwaiti or allied armed resistance, not all Iraqi or Kuwaiti national forces.
civilian_area_note: Count only civilians physically remaining in occupied Kuwait after August-1990 outflow.
split_reason: Violence mode, status coding, and denominator logic differ fundamentally from the later coalition air and ground campaigns.
quality_note: Civilian killings are clearly in the high hundreds, but missing-person and combatant-status issues block a full DR.

geographic_scope: Mixed national theater: Iraq nationwide plus occupied Kuwait; military operations also staged from Saudi Arabia, Gulf waters, carriers, and Turkey-linked air nodes
theater_description: The civilian footprint is national Iraq plus civilians still present in occupied Kuwait. The military footprint includes coalition and Iraqi forces directly assigned to the air campaign, including some personnel at fixed military targets outside the Kuwait Theater of Operations.
geometry_method_note: National Iraq is justified because bombing extended beyond the KTO into Baghdad, Basra, Nasiriyya, and other fixed-target sites throughout Iraq.
military_area_note: Military exposure includes Iraqi troops in the KTO, coalition troops directly assigned to the theater, and a scenario-based allowance for personnel at attacked fixed military targets elsewhere in Iraq.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure includes Iraq's national population plus a reduced occupied-Kuwait population after refugee outflow.
split_reason: Fixed campaign dates, a distinct airpower violence mode, and the best numerator/denominator source coverage make this the strongest episode for a core DR estimate.
quality_note: Good but imperfect triangulation: troop presence is strong, civilian direct deaths converge to low-thousands, but Iraqi military deaths remain method-sensitive.

geographic_scope: Subnational/corridor-level: Kuwait liberation battles, southern Iraq approaches, and retreat corridors such as Highway 80/coastal routes
admin_units: retreat routes toward Basra and Rumaila
theater_description: This episode covers the 100-hour ground offensive, including breaching operations, maneuver battles in Kuwait and southern Iraq, and attacks on retreating Iraqi columns.
geometry_method_note: A future civilian denominator would need a corridor-level or city-level battle-space polygon; whole-Iraq or whole-Kuwait civilian populations would be misleading.
military_area_note: Military exposure is concentrated in maneuver corridors and retreat routes rather than all forces nominally present in the broader theater.
civilian_area_note: Civilian presence was sparse in much of the battle space, but retreat columns may have included civilians or hostages in civilian-type vehicles.
split_reason: Ground combat and retreat-column violence have a different geography and casualty-status problem than the air campaign.
quality_note: Military deaths can be bounded, but civilian/unknown-status deaths and exposed civilian population remain too unresolved for a core DR.
